name: Corvus
description: Multi-agent AI research system — finds academic papers via semantic search & citation snowballing, then answers questions over them using agentic RAG with self-reflection. Built with LangGraph, FastAPI, Celery, and Qdrant.

commit.message:
docs(readme): fix architecture description and section headings

- Opening line: describe Corvus as a multi-agent system, not a paper discovery tool
- Agent architecture: replace "three-tier hierarchy" with accurate three-agent description
- Rename "Paper Finder Subgraph" and "Q&A Subgraph" headings to "Agent" for consistency
- Fix replan_agent formatting typo

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(readme): fix inconsistencies in agent and ingestion descriptions

- Paper finder: remove "from Semantic Scholar" — agent selects tools dynamically
- qa_retrieve: document unindexed paper detection added in finetune-qa-agent
- qa_answer: update to answer-first framing, single caveat for unindexed papers
- Ingestion: remove abstract-only fallback (removed), add shared vector store
  deduplication, parent indexing, and clean failure behaviour

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: Update README with asset link and demo info

Added a link to an asset and updated demo information.

verification.payload:
tree a74e11f87701399b75f0a6803c2ee8f095dff4ad
parent 50f727c8f0aac903498dd1470cfac2a75e09dedb
author Tim Yuan <61521613+YS0meone@users.noreply.github.com> 1772317475 -0600
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1772317475 -0600

chore: Update README with asset link and demo info

Added a link to an asset and updated demo information.

commit.message:
fix(build): comment out unused imports and state for hidden tool calls toggle

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(agent): extract paper_finder prompts and reorganize prompts.py

Move all inline prompts from paper_finder.py (planner, replan, search
agent, executor) into prompts.py as named constants. Reorganize the
file into clearly separated sections per module: paper_finder, graph,
qa, and evaluation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(ui): comment out Hide Tool Calls toggle

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(agent): extract inline prompts from graph.py into prompts.py

Move SEARCH_OPTIMIZATION_SYSTEM, QA_QUERY_OPTIMIZATION_SYSTEM,
QUERY_EVALUATION_SYSTEM, and PLANNER_SYSTEM into the central prompts
module, consistent with the pattern used by qa.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(auth): use custom fetch to inject Clerk token per-request

Replace the polling setInterval + clerkToken state approach with a
custom fetch wrapper (callerOptions.fetch) that calls getToken() lazily
before every LangGraph SDK request. This fixes 403 "Signature has
expired" errors caused by useStream caching its Client with stale
defaultHeaders — the new approach never holds a token snapshot, so
there's no expiry window to race against.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(qa): improve QA reliability and fix ingestion/config issues (#13)

* chore(config): support multiple env files via ENV_FILE

- Update .gitignore patterns: .env* (all variants) ignored, _example excluded
- config.py reads ENV_FILE env var to select which .env file to load
  - default: .env.local (local Docker services)
  - switch: ENV_FILE=.env.cloud uv run langgraph dev

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add Makefile and update Quick Start docs

Makefile targets: dev, dev-cloud, worker, worker-cloud, frontend, infra
README Quick Start now uses make commands and .env.local workflow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ingest): specific error messages and clean failure path

- arXiv search failure: "arXiv search failed — <reason>"
- Paper not on arXiv: "Not available on arXiv — upload the PDF manually to enable Q&A"
- PDF processing failure: "PDF processing failed — <reason>"
- Flatten nested try/except for cleaner control flow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(qa): detect unindexed papers upfront and pass to evaluator/answer nodes

On first retrieval iteration, check Qdrant for each selected paper and
record unindexed_paper_ids in state. Evaluator no longer loops for papers
with no full text; answer node adds a single end-of-response caveat instead
of leading with limitations. Prompts rewritten to answer-first framing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(tools): wrap QdrantService init inside asyncio.to_thread closure

QdrantService.__init__ makes a blocking socket call (collection_exists).
Moving instantiation inside the _search() closure ensures it runs in the
worker thread rather than on the event loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(config): inject API keys into os.environ via model_post_init

pydantic-settings loads keys from the env file into Settings fields, but
LangChain and OpenAI SDKs read os.environ directly. model_post_init now
pushes OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, and TAVILY_API_KEY into os.environ
so both the LangGraph server and Celery worker pick them up automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui): correct ingestion status display and show specific error messages

A Celery task with state=SUCCESS and result.success=false was incorrectly
shown as ingested. isSuccess now excludes result.success===false. Failed
rows now display the backend error string as a red subtitle so users know
why ingestion failed (e.g. not on arXiv, PDF processing failed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(dev): add --allow-blocking flag to langgraph dev commands

asyncio.to_thread propagates the contextvars.Context, so LangGraph's
blockbuster detector (ContextVar-based) still fires in worker threads.
--allow-blocking disables blockbuster in local dev; production uses
BG_JOB_ISOLATED_LOOPS=true on Fly.io / LangGraph Cloud.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(config): forward LangSmith tracing vars into os.environ

LangChain reads LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2, LANGCHAIN_API_KEY, LANGCHAIN_PROJECT,
and LANGCHAIN_ENDPOINT directly from os.environ. These vars are now declared
in Settings (so pydantic-settings picks them up from the env file) and pushed
into os.environ in model_post_init alongside the other API keys.

Also fixes the typo LANGSMITH_TRACING_V2 -> LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2 in
backend/.env_example.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(mcp): add Semantic Scholar MCP server

Exposes four academic search tools via the Model Context Protocol:
- search_papers: keyword + filter search over 200M+ papers
- get_paper: fetch full metadata by Semantic Scholar ID
- forward_snowball: find papers that cite a seed paper
- backward_snowball: find papers referenced by a seed paper

Connects to Claude Desktop, Cursor, and any MCP-compatible client.
Standalone setup in mcp/ with its own pyproject.toml (mcp, httpx, dotenv).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: correct model name and API key across all documentation

- GOOGLE_API_KEY → GEMINI_API_KEY in backend/.env_example and backend/README.md
- Model defaults: gemini-2.5-flash-preview-04-17 → google_genai:gemini-3-flash-preview
- QA_EVALUATOR_MODEL_NAME default → google_genai:gemini-3-pro-preview
- QDRANT_VECTOR_SIZE default: 768 → 1536
- Ingestion pipeline: allenai-specter → text-embedding-3-small

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: rename project to Corvus, expand agent architecture in README

- Rename all README titles from "AI Researcher Proto" to "Corvus"
- Rewrite root README with detailed three-tier agent architecture:
  supervisor pipeline (query_evaluation → planner → executor →
  post_tool → replanner), paper finder subgraph (planner/executor/
  replan_agent loop with Cohere reranking), Q&A subgraph (retrieve/
  evaluate/answer loop with scoped vector search)
- Document all tools, state machines, ingestion pipeline steps,
  and real-time UI features in depth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(worker): delete temp PDF after ingestion to prevent disk accumulation

The downloaded PDF was never cleaned up, causing the papers directory to
grow unboundedly and contributing to OOM pressure on the Grobid container.

Wrap add_s2_paper in a try/finally so pdf_path.unlink() runs whether
ingestion succeeds, raises, or falls through to the abstract fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(agent): inject current date into planner and search agent prompts

The LLM was generating stale year ranges (e.g. 2023-2025) for "recent"
queries because it has no awareness of the current date.

Inject date.today() into both the planner and search_agent_node system
prompts so year filters and "recent" interpretations are always accurate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(agent): preserve task constraints in planner step descriptions

Two issues caused the planner to silently drop the "recent" qualifier:

1. The web search tool description said "find famous/seminal papers" —
   a template bias that overrides the actual task, replacing "recent"
   with "highly cited or seminal" (different meaning). Changed to
   "discover relevant papers matching the task requirements".

2. No instruction to carry constraints forward into step descriptions.
   Added explicit guideline: time ranges, author names, venue filters,
   and other qualifiers from the task must appear in every step they
   apply to, and must not be substituted or weakened.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(agent): prevent search agent from treating web search mentions as retrieved papers

The search_agent_node was exiting early after a database search step because
paper titles mentioned in a prior Tavily summary appeared in the "Completed
Steps" context, making it think those papers were already in the list.

search_agent_node prompt:
- Add explicit callout that Completed Steps are action summaries only;
  only papers under "Current papers in your list" have been retrieved
- Tighten exit condition: for database/citation steps the goal is only met
  after actually calling s2_search_papers and receiving results

replan_agent prompt:
- Add critical note that web search mentions ≠ retrieved papers; the
  Papers list is the only ground truth for retrieval success
- Fix goal-achievement rule: specific author/paper lookup requires ≥1 paper
  in the Papers list, not just a web search naming the paper
- Add guidance to make database steps explicit about retrieval being the goal
  when a prior web search already named the target papers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(ui): add QA agent status card, generalize SubagentStatus component

Refactor PaperFinderStatus into a reusable SubagentStatus component with
title and icon props ('search' | 'qa'). Expose FinderStatus and QAStatus
as thin wrappers so the backend event names stay unchanged.

Register qa_status in the LoadExternalComponent map alongside finder_status.

Wire up qa_status events in the QA subgraph:
- qa_retrieve: emits running on entry, persists qa_ui_tracking_id in state
- qa_evaluate: emits running then completed (with description) on each branch
- qa_answer: emits running then completed with evidence count

Switch retrieve_and_answer_question from ainvoke to astream and forward
custom events into the supervisor stream (same pattern as find_papers).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: rewrite README and .env files to match current stack

Remove stale references to Elasticsearch, Kibana, QASPER evaluation,
ArXiv data pipeline, and hybrid/keyword/semantic search tools.

Update to reflect the current stack: Semantic Scholar API, Tavily web
search, Cohere reranking, Celery + Redis async ingestion, Grobid PDF
extraction, Qdrant vector store, Postgres checkpoints, and Clerk auth.

- backend/.env_example: full rewrite with all 23 vars from config.py
- .env.example (root): replace ES/Kibana config with infra defaults comment
- README.md (root): new stack table, quick start, agent architecture diagram
- backend/README.md: env var tables, supervisor/PF/QA flow, project structure
- web/README.md: add env vars table, document real-time UI features

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(agent): improve replan_agent goal criteria and surface plan context to search agent

replan_agent: add concrete goal-achieved heuristics (≥1 paper for specific
lookups, ≥5 for broad topics) and bias toward marking done to avoid over-searching.
Trim redundant planning guidelines that don't apply to the replanning decision.

search_agent_node: show upcoming plan steps in the system prompt so the agent
knows what work belongs to later steps and avoids pre-empting them. Also fix
two typos ("indentify" → "identify", "tool call" → "tool calls").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(tools): correct forward/backward snowball naming to match academic convention

Previously the names were inverted vs. standard systematic review terminology:
- forward_snowball was finding references (what seeds cite) = conventional backward
- backward_snowball was finding citing papers = conventional forward

Now:
- forward_snowball: finds papers that CITE the seeds (newer work, conventional forward)
- backward_snowball: traces what seeds CITE (foundations/references, conventional backward)

Updates function names, schema class names, docstrings, error messages, and all
prompt descriptions across paper_finder.py, paper_finder_fast.py, and search.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(agent): add PaperFinderStatus real-time UI for paper_finder subagent

- Add ui_tracking_id to PaperFinderState for stable in-place UI updates
- Emit finder_status push_ui_message at each phase of paper_finder
  (planner, executor, replan_agent) with running/completed transitions
- Fix event forwarding in find_papers: unwrap inner name/props instead
  of double-wrapping the ui event envelope
- Anchor finder_status UI to the already-committed tool-caller AIMessage
  so updates render in real-time during the tool call, not just after
- Add PaperFinderStatus React component (search icon, subagent chip)
- Register finder_status in LoadExternalComponent components map
- Rename StepTracker header from "Agent Actions" to "Supervisor Agent"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(agent): restrict planner/replanner to concrete search actions only

Add explicit rule that every plan step must be a search action (web,
database, or citation chasing). Prohibit review/filter/compare steps
since result ranking is handled automatically by Cohere reranking.
Also fix the fallback plan which contained a stray "review" step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(agent): rename outer search_agent to executor in paper_finder

Disambiguates the outer plan-step runner from the inner search_agent
subgraph, making graph traces easier to follow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(agent): remove dead code from paper_finder

Remove unused tool_node, should_clarify, and should_continue — none
were reachable in the graph.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(agent): improve get_paper_info_text formatting

Add include_abstract parameter (default True). Format authors as first
3 names + et al., remove paper ID, use clean bullet-list layout.
Planner and replanner use include_abstract=False since they only need
titles to track progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(agent): prevent search_task from over-specifying user intent

Add instruction to faithfully reflect only what the user asked for
and not introduce extra sub-topics or angles the user did not mention.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(agent): restrict snowball in fast paper finder prompt

Add explicit note to never use citation chasing for specific paper
queries unless the user asks for it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(agent): restrict snowball to explicit user requests in planner prompts

Remove speculative citation-chasing examples from both planner and
replanner system prompts. Add explicit rules to use snowball only when
the user asks for related/citing/cited papers, and minimize plan steps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(agent): split optimized_query into search_task + rerank_query

Keyword-soup queries confuse the planner into generating over-engineered
multi-step plans (web search + S2 search + snowball) for simple requests
like "find me the PaperQA paper". Natural-language task descriptions let
the planner choose the right strategy (e.g. a single title search).

- Replace _optimize_for_search with _optimize_for_paper_search, a single
  LLM call that returns both fields to avoid adding latency
- search_task: self-contained natural-language goal → feeds planner,
  replan_agent, and search agent; enables targeted strategy selection
- rerank_query: keyword-optimized string → used only by rerank_papers /
  rerank_node for semantic relevance scoring against paper abstracts
- PaperFinderState / SearchAgentState: replace optimized_query with the
  two new fields in both paper_finder.py and paper_finder_fast.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(agent): replace new_papers+ClearItem with flexible_reducer, reranker as plain function

- Add dedup-on-append to flexible_reducer so state["papers"] is always
  clean when the agent sees it; no more manual deduplication scattered
  across nodes
- Remove ClearItem sentinel and new_paper_reducer entirely
- paper_finder.py: rerank_node -> rerank_papers(papers, query) plain
  async function; outer search_agent calls it directly after graph
  completes instead of building a fake state dict
- paper_finder_fast.py: rerank_node stays as a graph node (legitimate
  position in edge chain) but Replace+flexible_reducer handle the
  reset correctly; internal dedup removed since reducer handles it
- Tools (s2_search_papers, forward_snowball, backward_snowball) now
  write to "papers" directly instead of "new_papers"
- Remove get_paper_details from both agents; inject current paper list
  into system prompt instead (saves an LLM round trip)
- Replace all print() calls with proper logging via getLogger(__name__)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(agent): make all paper_finder nodes async, guard Cohere init

- planner, replan_agent, search_agent_node, rerank_node, search_agent
  all converted to async with ainvoke / asyncio.to_thread
- Guard ranker init with COHERE_API_KEY check to prevent crash on import

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(web): restore loading skeleton for thread history sidebar

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(agent): rename query clarification node to query evaluation

- Rename StepName.QUERY_CLARIFICATION → QUERY_EVALUATION in schema.py
- Rename graph node and function query_clarification → query_evaluation
- Rename should_clarify → should_proceed edge routing function
- Replace positional is_clear bool arg with decision= keyword arg in
  UIManager.update_ui and get_update_query_evaluation_step, producing
  a distinct description for each of the 5 decision outcomes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(web): use getToken({skipCache:true}) to prevent stale Clerk JWT

Without skipCache, getToken() returns the same cached token until it
expires. Since the token string never changes, clerkToken state never
updates, useStream's useMemo client is never recreated, and requests
continue using the expired token → HTTP 403 "Signature has expired".

With skipCache:true, every poll mints a fresh JWT, the state always
changes, and useStream gets a valid token. Interval reduced to 45s
(15s buffer before the 60s expiry).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

debug(worker): add comprehensive GROBID diagnostics to add_s2_paper

Logs all known failure scenarios in one deployment:
1. What PDFs are on disk vs what the glob pattern expects (file name mismatch,
   special chars breaking Path.glob, wrong directory)
2. File size of matched PDF (detects empty/corrupt download)
3. Raw GROBID POST response (status code, content-type, preview) bypassing
   the LangChain wrapper that silently swallows non-200 responses

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(worker): make CLERK_JWKS_URL optional with empty default

The Celery worker doesn't verify JWTs — only the LangGraph backend does.
Requiring CLERK_JWKS_URL in Settings caused a ValidationError on worker
startup since it's not set in fly.worker.toml or worker secrets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): replace Windows backslashes in .dockerignore with forward slashes

The file was auto-generated by flyctl on Windows using **\ patterns.
On the Linux remote builder these are a syntax error, causing:
  "error from sender: failed to match excludepatterns: syntax error in pattern"
Replaced all **\ with **/ and cleaned up the file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): run flyctl from backend/ so uv.lock is in the build context

flyctl uses the working directory as the Docker build context, not the
directory of --config. Running from repo root meant Docker looked for
uv.lock at the root instead of backend/. Setting working-directory: backend
fixes the build context so all files referenced in Dockerfile.worker exist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): track uv.lock so Docker build has it in the build context

uv.lock was in .gitignore so GitHub Actions never had the file,
causing 'flyctl deploy --remote-only' to fail with '/uv.lock not found'.
Lock files should be committed for reproducible builds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(worker): correct GROBID_SERVER_URL to full endpoint path

GrobidParser uses the URL directly as a POST target — it does not
append any path. The previous value '/api/proces' was both truncated
and missing the correct endpoint name, causing GROBID to return empty
responses (0 segments). Correct endpoint is /api/processFulltextDocument.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(web): add vercel.json to fix SPA routing 404s on page reload/redirect

Without this, Vercel returns 404 for any direct navigation to client-side
routes like /sign-in since no physical file exists at that path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(web): align afterSignOutUrl with explicit sign-out redirect

Prevents a double-redirect (/ → /sign-in) when signing out by matching
the ClerkProvider afterSignOutUrl to the redirectUrl used in the sign-out button.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(web): refresh Clerk token every 50 s to match 60 s JWT expiry

Clerk session JWTs expire after 60 seconds (not 60 minutes). The previous
55-minute interval meant the cached token passed as an Authorization header
was expired for most requests, causing HTTP 403 "Signature has expired".
getToken() transparently re-mints the JWT as needed; polling every 50 s
keeps clerkToken state and defaultHeaders always valid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: add Fly.io deploy workflow for Celery worker

Deploys ai-researcher-worker on push to main when backend/ files change.
Requires FLY_API_TOKEN secret in GitHub repository settings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Clerk authentication (frontend + backend) (#11)

* feat(web): add Clerk authentication with split-screen landing page

- Install @clerk/clerk-react and wrap app in ClerkProvider
- Add /sign-in and /sign-up routes with path-based routing so
  sign-up flow stays in-app rather than redirecting to Clerk's hosted pages
- Split-screen landing: dark left panel with headline, description,
  and feature callouts; Clerk form on the right
- Unauthenticated users are redirected to /sign-in; signed-in users
  see the Thread UI

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(web): add user section with sign-out to sidebar

Add a pinned user section at the bottom of the thread history sidebar
(desktop and mobile). Shows avatar initials, display name, and a
popover with email and sign-out action.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(auth): integrate Clerk JWT verification into LangGraph backend

- Add backend/auth.py (root-level) with async JWKS fetch via httpx to
  avoid blockbuster blocking errors; in-process key cache with 1h TTL
- Register auth handler in langgraph.json at ./auth.py to sidestep
  Windows path-separator bug that breaks langgraph up with subdir paths
- Add DISABLE_AUTH flag (default false) to bypass auth in local dev
  so LangGraph Studio can connect without a Clerk token
- Add CLERK_JWKS_URL and DISABLE_AUTH to config.py
- Frontend: fetch Clerk session token via useAuth().getToken() and
  pass as Authorization: Bearer header in both StreamProvider and
  ThreadProvider; update createClient to accept defaultHeaders

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* revert: restore auth.py to app/ and revert langgraph.json path

Remove the root-level auth.py workaround for langgraph up Windows path
issue — not needed since we deploy to LangSmith Cloud, not self-hosted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(schema): add CLEAR and UNCLEAR to StepStatus enum

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ui): update query clarification step to use CLEAR/UNCLEAR statuses

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(ui): replace CLEAR/UNCLEAR statuses with is_clear arg on COMPLETED

Instead of separate StepStatus variants, pass is_clear: bool to
get_update_query_clarification_step to select the description.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(web): move providers inside <SignedIn> to prevent auth race condition

StreamProvider, ThreadProvider, and PaperSelectionProvider are now only
mounted for authenticated users via an AuthenticatedApp wrapper component.
This prevents useStream from being initialized without an Authorization
header before the Clerk token is fetched. Also removes the now-deleted
duplicate backend/auth.py root-level file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: HITL paper selection, conditional interrupt & UI improvements (#10)

* feat(web): replace HITL continue/text-box with Yes/No buttons

- SelectPapersInterruptView now shows explicit "Yes, proceed" / "No, search again" buttons
  instead of relying on the shared text box for resuming
- "Yes" submits selected papers directly from the interrupt view
- "No" re-enables the text box (amber border, dimmed while locked) so the
  user can type a new search query, which resumes the graph with an empty
  paper selection and the new query as user_message
- Text box is disabled with notice "Please respond to the question above first..."
  while the interrupt is pending, preventing accidental sends

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(hitl): conditional interrupt + ingestion-gated proceed UI

- Backend: post_tool_route only routes to replanner when
  retrieve_and_answer_question remains in plan_steps; find-only
  plans now end without an interrupt
- Frontend: SelectPapersInterruptView now has amber warning state
  (no selection) and emerald ready state (papers selected), with
  explanatory copy guiding users to Add to list + wait for ingestion
  before proceeding
- Replanner simplified to deterministic path (no LLM replan call)
- Thread: removed interruptRejected state; input locked during any
  interrupt; ui.tsx checkbox toggles sync to global PaperSelection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(web): add select all/deselect all toggle to paper list

- Remove checkbox-to-context sync so papers only enter PaperSelection
  context (and My Paper List panel) via the Add to list button
- Add Select all / Deselect all toggle in the paper list header that
  updates local tempSelected state only

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(web): improve landing UI and paper list copy

- Landing header: left-align, enlarge Corvus SVG and title, add
  descriptive snippet mimicking Asta's style
- Placeholder: update to "Find papers, ask a question, or both…"
- Selected papers panel: clarify description to say papers are
  available to the research assistant

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(web): apply env var fallbacks in ThreadProvider for thread history

getThreads was returning [] when apiUrl/assistantId weren't in the URL
query string, even if VITE_API_URL/VITE_ASSISTANT_ID were set. Apply
the same env var fallbacks used by Stream.tsx so the sidebar populates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(web): improve StepTracker UI with hover effects and consistent styling

Redesign StepTracker to match the app's gray-scale palette:
- rounded-xl → rounded-lg to match paper list card
- Replace blue/emerald accents with gray-900 icons matching primary palette
- Add per-row hover effects via Tailwind group/hover utilities
- Header font matches paper list (text-base font-semibold text-gray-900)
- Replace colored badge with subtle plain-text running indicator
- Divide-y hairlines between steps instead of individual bordered rows

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(web): use VITE_API_URL for ingest endpoint instead of separate env var

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(web): set Corvus tab title and favicon

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
fix(grobid): fixed the OOM of grobid container

fix(worker): pass GROBID_SERVER_URL to all GrobidParser calls

add_papers_batch and add_s2_paper were still using the default
localhost:8070 instead of the configured GROBID_SERVER_URL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(deploy): switch grobid to CRF image, performance-1x, internal-only

- Use grobid/grobid:0.8.2-crf (matches docker-compose, ~500MB vs 8GB)
- Downsize to performance-1x with 2gb RAM (CRF needs <1GB)
- Remove [http_service] — no public endpoint, accessed only via
  Fly private network at ai-researcher-grobid.internal:8070

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(deploy): add fly.grobid.toml for GROBID server on Fly.io

Deploys lfoppiano/grobid:0.8.1 as a separate Fly app (ai-researcher-grobid)
on a performance-2x 8GB machine in iad. Accessible to the Celery worker via
private network at http://ai-researcher-grobid.internal:8070.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(backend): add GROBID_SERVER_URL env var to config and qdrant service

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
chore(frontend): added the social preview png
fix(frontend): fix the bug where the hide tool call button is not synced with the actual setting

feat(web): rebrand to Corvus, load config from env, improve thread history UI

- Rename VITE_BACKEND_API_URL → VITE_API_URL (fix silent mismatch), add
  VITE_ASSISTANT_ID and VITE_LANGSMITH_API_KEY to .env.example
- Remove config login form from StreamProvider; values always come from
  env vars / URL params with hardcoded fallbacks
- Add CorvusSVG icon component; replace LangGraph logo and rename
  "Agent Chat" → "Corvus" throughout thread UI
- Rewrite ThreadHistory: remove buggy skeleton loading state, highlight
  active thread, show relative dates, add empty state, tighten layout
- Default hideToolCalls to true

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(s2): replace semanticscholar lib with httpx async to fix uvloop incompatibility

The semanticscholar library internally calls nest_asyncio.apply() which
cannot patch uvloop.Loop (used by LangGraph Cloud), causing:
  "Error searching for papers: Can't patch loop of type <class 'uvloop.Loop'>"

Replace with direct async httpx calls to the S2 REST API. Make
s2_search_papers, forward_snowball, and backward_snowball tool functions
async. Remove semanticscholar and nest-asyncio from dependencies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf(worker): re-enable Celery heartbeat/mingle/gossip on Redis Cloud flat-rate

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(deploy): tell setuptools to only package app/, not eval/

Prevents "Multiple top-level packages discovered" error in LangGraph Cloud build.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(deploy): lower requires-python to >=3.11 for LangGraph Cloud compatibility

LangGraph Cloud build image uses Python 3.11; relax constraint from >=3.12.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(deploy): fix LangGraph Cloud dependency path to install from pyproject.toml

Change dependencies from ["./app"] to ["."] so LangGraph Cloud installs
all packages from backend/pyproject.toml (including fastapi, celery, etc.)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf(worker): disable Celery heartbeat/mingle/gossip to reduce Redis traffic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(config): remove auto-loaded env vars from Settings, switch to extra=ignore

Remove LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2, LANGCHAIN_API_KEY, LANGCHAIN_PROJECT,
OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, TAVILY_API_KEY — these are read
directly from os.environ by their respective SDKs and don't need
explicit Settings fields. Switch extra="forbid" to extra="ignore"
so unrecognised env vars don't cause validation errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(deploy): add Fly.io worker deployment config

- Add Dockerfile.worker for Celery worker container
- Add fly.worker.toml with 2GB RAM, concurrency=1, and all required env vars
- Add backend/.dockerignore (with uv.lock unblocked for Docker builds)
- Remove langgraph.sh startup script (no longer needed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(qdrant): create payload index for Qdrant Cloud strict mode compatibility

Qdrant Cloud enforces strict mode (unindexed_filtering_retrieve=False),
rejecting any filter on fields without a payload index. Create a keyword
index on metadata.id at startup (idempotent) so paper existence checks
and filtered searches work on both local and Cloud instances.

Also fix TaskStatus to handle failed tasks where result is an exception,
not a dict.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(worker): load .env into os.environ at Celery startup

Celery worker doesn't auto-load .env unlike langgraph dev, causing SDKs
that read from os.environ (OpenAIEmbeddings, Gemini, etc.) to fail on import.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ingest): add Qdrant API key support and prevent AsyncResult event-loop leak

- Add QDRANT_API_KEY config field (optional, defaults to empty for local)
  and pass it through QdrantConfig to QdrantClient for Qdrant Cloud auth
- Fix blockbuster BlockingError: AsyncResult.__del__ now always fires inside
  the thread by extracting primitive values and calling del before returning

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(config): clean up env vars and model names for deployment

- Remove unused config groups: Elasticsearch, LOADER_BATCH/WORKERS/etc,
  DATABASE_*, POSTGRES_* (11 vars total)
- Replace generic AGENT_MODEL_NAME/GEMINI_MODEL_NAME with per-agent vars:
  SUPERVISOR_MODEL_NAME, PF_AGENT_MODEL_NAME, PF_FILTER_MODEL_NAME,
  QA_AGENT_MODEL_NAME, QA_EVALUATION_MODEL_NAME, QA_EVALUATOR_MODEL_NAME,
  QA_BASELINE_MODEL_NAME
- Add EMBEDDING_MODEL_NAME and REDIS_URL to replace hard-coded values
- Rename LOADER_OUTPUT_DIR -> PDF_DOWNLOAD_DIR, LANGSMITH_TRACING_V2 -> LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2
- Remove explicit api_key args from all init_chat_model calls
- Remove model_post_init (no longer needed), drop unused os/Any imports
- Reuse module-level supervisor_model in _optimize_for_search/_optimize_for_qa
- Split qa_eval_model from qa_model for the evaluation step

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(scripts): add langgraph.sh startup script with Postgres checkpointer URI

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(infra): add Postgres to Docker Compose for local LangGraph checkpointing

LangGraph Platform injects a checkpointer automatically when given a
Postgres URI. Add a postgres:16-alpine service so threads persist across
langgraph dev restarts locally.

Start with: docker compose up -d
Run backend with: uv run langgraph dev --postgres-uri postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/langgraph

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ingest): resolve Qdrant ReadTimeout on concurrent paper ingestion

Three issues combined to cause the timeout crash:
- QdrantClient had no timeout configured (defaulted to 5s), too short
  under concurrent load
- check_paper_exists used exact=True forcing a full collection scan
- autoretry_for was missing so max_retries never actually triggered

Fix: set client timeout to 60s, switch to approximate count, and add
autoretry_for with exponential backoff + jitter so transient network
errors retry automatically instead of crashing the task.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf(agent): convert graph nodes and tools to async

All LLM calls were synchronous, blocking the event loop under concurrent
users and serializing all requests. Convert every graph node and the
document filter tool to async def with ainvoke. Wrap the Cohere reranker
and Qdrant client (sync-only APIs) in asyncio.to_thread.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
evaluation qa agent wrapper bug fix
refactored the qa evaluation code
fixed the retrieval tool's concurrent write issue
qa graph improvement
Added planner and replanner test
refactor the main graph with plan and execute pattern and added hitl for post paper finding review
decoupled the query optimization for qa and paper finder
optimized the paper list componet ui/ux. now it shows the ingestion progress and the ingestion status
major refactory. get rid of the legacy local paper search modules"
fixed the global ui anti pattern
bug fixes
Added ui manager to showcasing the ai workflow
Added agent action ui and modified logging
bug fixes, tested the new qa agent
wip qa refactor
Added paper list ui
ingestion pipeline bug fix
Added ingestion pipeline
Added paper ingestion endpoint
added ingest api
updated the supervisor logic

commit.message: succesfully added generative ui component to show the paper list
added the paper component in the frontend
Added a fast version of paper finder
using supervisor pattern to connect the paper finder and qa asnwer agent
added snowball tools
fine tuned paper finder prompt

readme_html:
Corvus

A multi-agent AI research system built on a supervisor architecture. A top-level supervisor routes between specialized subagents — a Paper Finding Agent for academic search and citation discovery, and a Q&A Agent for evidence-based question answering over the papers you select.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bfd8e9bc-2266-42f7-9a8e-5412029f08fc

Live demo → corvus-agent.vercel.app | MCP server — use Corvus's search tools directly in Claude Desktop

Stack

Layer Technology
Agent orchestration LangGraph (supervisor + subgraph pattern)
LLM inference Google Gemini 3 Flash (preview) via LangChain
API server FastAPI
Async task queue Celery + Redis
Frontend React 19 + Vite + TypeScript
Vector database Qdrant
Relational / checkpoints Postgres
PDF extraction Grobid
Paper search Semantic Scholar API (200M+ papers)
Web search Tavily
Reranking Cohere
Embeddings OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (1536-dim)
Auth Clerk (optional)

Agent Architecture

Corvus is built around three LangGraph agents: a Supervisor that plans and routes, a Paper Finding Agent that searches and discovers papers, and a Q&A Agent that retrieves evidence and answers questions.

Supervisor (graph.py)

Every user message enters the supervisor, which runs a four-node pipeline before invoking any tool:

query_evaluation → planner → executor → supervisor_tools → post_tool
                                                                ↓
                                                    replanner (interrupt)
                                                                ↓
                                                           executor …

**query_evaluation** — Classifies the query before any search is attempted. Uses structured output to return one of five decisions: clear, needs_clarification, unselected_paper, irrelevant, or inappropriate. Only clear queries proceed; all others generate an inline response explaining what Corvus needs.

**planner** — Chooses one of three execution plans based on intent:

  • find_only — discover new papers
  • qa_only — answer a question using papers already in context
  • find_then_qa — find papers first, then answer a question about them

**executor** — Pops the next step from the plan and issues the corresponding tool call (find_papers or retrieve_and_answer_question) as a structured AIMessage with a tool call.

**post_tool** — Runs after every tool result to commit side effects (paper list UI, final answer) to state before any interrupt is issued, ensuring the checkpoint is consistent.

**replanner** — Issues a LangGraph interrupt("select_papers") so the user can choose which papers to carry into Q&A. On resume, the selected paper IDs and an optional follow-up message are extracted from the resume payload and committed to state.

Paper Finding Agent (paper_finder.py)

Invoked by the supervisor's find_papers tool. Runs an iterative search loop (max 3 iterations) using a combination of search tools:

planner ──→ executor ──→ replan_agent
   ↑                          │
   └──── (goal not met) ──────┘
                              │
                        (goal met) ──→ END

**planner** — Analyses the search task and generates a concrete multi-step search plan. Each step must be an actionable search operation (web search, academic database query, or citation chasing). The planner is given today's date so temporal constraints like "recent" resolve correctly.

**executor** — Runs a nested ReAct-style search agent for the current plan step. The search agent has access to four tools:

Tool Description
s2_search_papers Keyword and filter search over Semantic Scholar (year, venue, citation count, etc.)
tavily_research_overview Web search for broader context, seminal papers, and author profiles
forward_snowball Find papers that cite a given seed paper (surfaces recent follow-on work)
backward_snowball Find papers cited by a given seed paper (surfaces foundational references)

After the search agent finishes its step, all accumulated papers are reranked with Cohere against a keyword-optimised query derived from the original task. Only the top-35 most relevant papers are kept, preventing the list from growing unboundedly across iterations.

**replan_agent** — Evaluates whether the goal is achieved by inspecting the actual paper list (not just what appeared in web search summaries). If the goal is not yet met, it rewrites the remaining plan steps with explicit retrieval targets, then loops back to the executor.

All three nodes emit finder_status custom events that the frontend renders as a real-time Research Agent status card.

Q&A Agent (qa.py)

Invoked after paper selection. Performs iterative evidence retrieval scoped to the user-selected paper IDs (max 3 iterations):

qa_retrieve ──→ tools ──→ qa_evaluate ──→ qa_answer
                               │
                   (insufficient evidence)
                               │
                          qa_retrieve …

**qa_retrieve** — On the first iteration, checks Qdrant for each selected paper and records which ones have no indexed full text (e.g. ingestion failed or paper not on arXiv). These unindexed_paper_ids are carried in state so downstream nodes can handle thin evidence gracefully. Then generates focused retrieval queries and calls retrieve_evidence_from_selected_papers, which performs vector search over the Qdrant collection filtered to the selected paper IDs only. This scoping ensures answers are grounded exclusively in the papers the user chose.

**qa_evaluate** — Uses structured output to decide between two outcomes: AnswerQuestion (evidence is sufficient) or AskForMoreEvidence (evidence is weak — includes a limitation string that seeds the next retrieval attempt with a better query strategy).

**qa_answer** — Synthesises a final answer from all accumulated evidence chunks. Always leads with the answer; if any selected papers were unindexed, a single caveat sentence is appended at the end noting that only the abstract was available for those papers.

All three nodes emit qa_status events rendered as a real-time Q&A Agent status card.

Paper Ingestion Pipeline (Celery)

When a user adds a paper to their list, two things happen simultaneously: the paper is added to the agent's context (so it knows which papers the Q&A should be scoped to), and an ingestion task is pushed to the Redis queue for background processing.

The vector store is shared across all users. The Celery worker checks whether the paper is already indexed before doing any work — if another user ingested it earlier, the task skips straight to done. This significantly reduces average ingestion time across the platform.

If the paper is not yet indexed, the worker runs the full pipeline:

  1. arXiv lookup — searches arXiv by title to find a PDF URL
  2. PDF download — fetches the PDF to a temp file (PDF_DOWNLOAD_DIR)
  3. Grobid parsing — sends the PDF to a local Grobid container for structured, section-aware full-text extraction
  4. Chunking & embedding — splits extracted text using a parent indexing strategy: smaller chunks are embedded for accurate vector retrieval; larger parent chunks are stored for richer LLM context at answer time. Embeddings use OpenAI text-embedding-3-small.
  5. Qdrant upsert — stores vectors with paper ID and section metadata for scoped retrieval
  6. Cleanup — the temp PDF is deleted regardless of success or failure

If no arXiv PDF is found, the task returns a clean failure with a specific error message. No partial data is written to Qdrant, so re-ingestion can be attempted cleanly.

UI Features

  • Real-time subagent status cards — live in-place updates as the Research Agent and Q&A Agent run (planning, searching, evaluating, generating)
  • Paper selection interrupt — after search completes, a structured interrupt pauses execution so the user can choose which papers to bring into Q&A
  • Ingestion progress panel — per-paper status tracking with a progress bar as the Celery worker processes each PDF in the background
  • Streaming AI responses — token-by-token streaming via the LangGraph SDK
  • Persistent thread history — conversation threads are stored in Postgres and restored across sessions

Quick Start

1. Clone

git clone https://github.com/YS0meone/Corvus.git
cd Corvus

2. Configure environment

cp backend/.env_example backend/.env.local

Edit backend/.env.local and fill in at minimum:

  • OPENAI_API_KEY — for embeddings
  • GEMINI_API_KEY — for all agent LLM calls
  • COHERE_API_KEY — for reranking
  • S2_API_KEY — for Semantic Scholar search

Local infrastructure variables (Qdrant, Redis, Grobid) are pre-filled with localhost defaults.

3. Start infrastructure

make infra

Starts Redis (6379), Qdrant (6333), and Grobid (8070) via Docker Compose.

4. Start the Celery worker, backend, and frontend

Open three terminals and run:

make worker    # Celery worker
make dev       # LangGraph backend  (http://localhost:2024)
make frontend  # React frontend     (http://localhost:5173)

Open http://localhost:5173.

Switching to cloud services

To run against your deployed Qdrant, Redis, and Grobid instead:

cp backend/.env_example backend/.env.cloud
# fill in cloud service URLs and API keys

make dev-cloud
make worker-cloud

  • [backend/README.md](./backend/README.md) — environment variables, project structure, full architecture reference
  • [web/README.md](./web/README.md) — frontend setup and env vars
